parameters.jsCode:
const uploadedDocs = ($json.uploadedFiles || []).map(file => ({
  type: "document",
  source: {
    type: "file",
    file_id: file.fileId,
  }
}));

const xmlTexts = ($json.xmlFiles || []).map(file => ({
  type: "text",
  text: `Filename: ${file.fileName}\n\n${file.xmlText}`
}));

return [{
  json: {
    gmailMessageId: $json.gmailMessageId,
    anthropicContent: [
      ...uploadedDocs,
      ...xmlTexts,
      {
        type: "text",
        text: `Analyze all attached documents together.

Goal:
Determine whether these attachments contain an incoming supplier invoice.
If yes, extract the invoice data for work4all.

Rules:
- Return ONLY valid JSON.
- Use this exact top-level shape:
  {
    "found_invoice": true,
    "reason": "short explanation",
    "relevantAttachmentKeys": ["attachment_0"],
    "invoiceData": {
      "supplierCode": null,
      "supplierName": null,
      "supplierCustomerNumberAtSupplier": null,
      "supplierContactMailAddress": null,
      "supplierIban": null,
      "projectCode": null,
      "projectNumber": null,
      "projectName": null,
      "invoiceNumberSupplier": null,
      "note": null,
      "invoiceDate": null,
      "entryDate": null,
      "receiptDate": null,
      "paymentTermDays": null,
      "discount1Rate": null,
      "discount1Days": null,
      "discount2Rate": null,
      "discount2Days": null,
      "currencyCode": null,
      "invoiceItems": []
    }
  }

Extraction rules:
- found_invoice = true only if at least one attachment is clearly an incoming invoice / supplier invoice / receipt for accounts payable.
- relevantAttachmentKeys must contain the original attachment keys, e.g. "attachment_0".
- invoiceData field names must match exactly.
- Dates must be ISO-8601 strings.
- Do not invent values.
- Prefer supplier matching fields in this order when available:
  supplierCode > supplierName > supplierCustomerNumberAtSupplier > supplierContactMailAddress > supplierIban
- Prefer project matching fields in this order when available:
  projectCode > projectNumber > projectName
- invoiceItems must NOT contain all raw positions.
- Instead, group and aggregate positions by VAT rate (Mehrwertsteuersatz).
- Return one invoiceItems entry per VAT bucket.
- For each VAT bucket, sum:
  - netAmount
  - vatAmount
  - grossAmount
- Only set account, costCenter, costGroup, projectCode, taxCode if explicitly present in the source.
- If no invoice is found:
  - found_invoice = false
  - relevantAttachmentKeys = []
  - invoiceData = { all fields null, invoiceItems: [] }`
      }
    ]
  }
}];

parameters.jsonBody:
={{
{
  model: "claude-sonnet-4-6",
  max_tokens: 10000,
  messages: [
    {
      role: "user",
      content: [
        ...$json.anthropicContent,
        {
          type: "text",
          text: `
Return ONLY valid JSON.
Do not use markdown.
Do not wrap in triple backticks.
Do not add explanations before or after the JSON.

Use exactly this shape:
{
  "found_invoice": true,
  "reason": "short explanation",
  "relevantAttachmentKeys": ["attachment_0"],
  "invoiceData": {
    "supplierCode": null,
    "supplierName": null,
    "supplierCustomerNumberAtSupplier": null,
    "supplierContactMailAddress": null,
    "supplierIban": null,
    "projectCode": null,
    "projectNumber": null,
    "projectName": null,
    "invoiceNumberSupplier": null,
    "note": null,
    "invoiceDate": null,
    "entryDate": null,
    "receiptDate": null,
    "paymentTermDays": null,
    "discount1Rate": null,
    "discount1Days": null,
    "discount2Rate": null,
    "discount2Days": null,
    "currencyCode": null,
    "invoiceItems": [
      {
        "account": null,
        "costCenter": null,
        "costGroup": null,
        "projectCode": null,
        "taxCode": null,
        "taxRate": null,
        "netAmount": null,
        "grossAmount": null,
        "vatAmount": null,
        "note": null
      }
    ]
  }
}

Rules:
- found_invoice = true only if at least one attachment is clearly an incoming supplier invoice / Eingangsrechnung / XRechnung / receipt relevant for accounting.
- relevantAttachmentKeys must contain the original attachment keys like "attachment_0".
- invoiceData field names must match exactly.
- Dates must be ISO-8601 strings if known, otherwise null.
- Do not invent values.
- Aggregate invoiceItems by VAT rate (Mehrwertsteuersatz), not by raw line item.
- Return one invoiceItems entry per VAT bucket.
- Sum netAmount, vatAmount and grossAmount per VAT bucket.
- Only fill account, costCenter, costGroup, projectCode, taxCode if explicitly present in the documents.
- If no invoice is found, return:
  {
    "found_invoice": false,
    "reason": "...",
    "relevantAttachmentKeys": [],
    "invoiceData": {
      "supplierCode": null,
      "supplierName": null,
      "supplierCustomerNumberAtSupplier": null,
      "supplierContactMailAddress": null,
      "supplierIban": null,
      "projectCode": null,
      "projectNumber": null,
      "projectName": null,
      "invoiceNumberSupplier": null,
      "note": null,
      "invoiceDate": null,
      "entryDate": null,
      "receiptDate": null,
      "paymentTermDays": null,
      "discount1Rate": null,
      "discount1Days": null,
      "discount2Rate": null,
      "discount2Days": null,
      "currencyCode": null,
      "invoiceItems": []
    }
  }
`
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
}}

nodes.name: Aggregate multiple Attachments back to one Email
